name: Build 20260329 (20416fff) - ac12g
body:
Automated firmware build

**Router model:** ac12g
**Source commit:** 20416fff4b7eb90a0f0b5d1c14404212d33fbedf
**Build date:** 2026.03.29_21.47.00

Last commit message: fix: race condition in jp_spec_setup causing use-after-free  mutex_init(&spec->lock) was called in jp_spec_setup while another thread could be holding the lock in jp_spec_applymods. Re-initializing a locked mutex corrupts its state, allowing both threads to enter the critical section simultaneously.  This caused use-after-free when: 1. send.c holds spec->lock, uses spec->pkt 2. jp_spec_setup re-inits the locked mutex (broken state) 3. jp_spec_setup acquires the \"lock\" immediately 4. jp_spec_setup does kfree(spec->pkt) 5. send.c continues using freed memory -> crash  Fix: move mutex_init to wg_newlink where it's called once during device creation, before any concurrent access.

name: Build 20260329 (20416fff) - tplink-c5-v4-16M
body:

Last commit message: fix: race condition in jp_spec_setup causing use-after-free  mutex_init(&spec->lock) was called in jp_spec_setup while another thread could be holding the lock in jp_spec_applymods. Re-initializing a locked mutex corrupts its state, allowing both threads to enter the critical section simultaneously.  This caused use-after-free when: 1. send.c holds spec->lock, uses spec->pkt 2. jp_spec_setup re-inits the locked mutex (broken state) 3. jp_spec_setup acquires the \"lock\" immediately 4. jp_spec_setup does kfree(spec->pkt) 5. send.c continues using freed memory -> crash  Fix: move mutex_init to wg_newlink where it's called once during device creation, before any concurrent access.

name: Build 20260323 (52ca792d) - tplink-c5-v4-16M
body:
Automated firmware build

**Router model:** tplink-c5-v4-16M
**Source commit:** 52ca792df6eebd97f44e16d8b92466f8957e1efc
**Build date:** 2026.03.24_01.28.40

Last commit message: redesigned the interface of the \"Services\" tab of the \"Administration\" menu The \"Services\" tab has been moved to the main menu and renamed to \"Management\". User services are now located in tabs.  Redesigned interface for dnscrypt, https_dns_proxy (DoH), stubby (DoT)  Additional DNS settings have been added to the DHPC server interface: cache size, all-servers, strict-order, filter-AAAA, ipv4 priority increase via gai.conf, forced redirection of all dns requests from the local network and vpn (clients of the wg or openvpn server) to the router's dns server.
